examples.title: Insertion Sort Algorithm
examples.description: Standard insertion sort algorithm that builds sorted array one element at a time
examples.code:
PROCEDURE InsertionSort(A[0..n-1])
BEGIN
    FOR i ← 1 TO n-1 DO
        key ← A[i]
        j ← i - 1
        
        WHILE j >= 0 AND A[j] > key DO
            A[j+1] ← A[j]
            j ← j - 1
        END WHILE
        
        A[j+1] ← key
    END FOR
END
examples.explanation: For each element (starting from index 1), store it as 'key'. Compare with elements to its left and shift all elements greater than key one position right. Finally place key in its correct position. The sorted portion grows from left to right.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) best, O(n²) average, O(n²) worst

examples.title: Selection Sort in Python
examples.description: Implementation of selection sort that finds minimum element in unsorted portion and places it at the beginning
examples.explanation: The outer loop runs n-1 times. In each iteration, we find the index of minimum element in the unsorted portion (from i to n-1). Then we swap the minimum element with the element at position i. After each pass, the sorted portion grows from the start.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n²) for all cases (best, average, worst)

examples.title: Optimized Bubble Sort in C
examples.description: Bubble sort with early termination optimization using a swapped flag
examples.explanation: The algorithm uses a 'swapped' flag that is set to 1 whenever a swap occurs. After each complete pass, if no swaps occurred, the array is already sorted and we break out of the loop. This improves best case from O(n²) to O(n) for already sorted arrays.

examples.title: Insertion Sort with Counters in Java
examples.description: Insertion sort implementation with comparison and shift counters for analysis
examples.explanation: This implementation tracks the number of comparisons and shifts made during sorting. This is useful for analyzing algorithm behavior on different inputs. The algorithm shifts elements greater than key one position right, then inserts key in its correct position.
